mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects robust aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. This is a persistent trend signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of GDP expansion are not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing manufacturing activity and supply-side expansion, which drives corporate profits and supports positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of months where INDPRO rises at least 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 5.0%, the labor market becomes unambiguously tight, leading to wage pressures and anticipatory Fed tightening that depresses equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) exceeding 275 signals persistent inflationary pressure that forces the Fed to raise interest rates, raising discount rates and reducing present value of future earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of cases when CPIAUCSL exceeds 275, or rank IC of CPIAUCSL level is above 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS > 200k) combined with the fed funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) indicates an overheating economy that will require future monetary tightening, hurting equities through higher discount rates over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of months that satisfy the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high retail sales (RSAFS > 600k) and high housing starts (HOUST > 1500k) reflect exceptionally strong aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of weeks satisfying the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: In the regime of accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation, defined by Fed funds near zero and CPI above 5% year-over-year, the market reprices risk as inflation erodes real earnings and future tightening becomes inevitable, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months that satisfy the regime condition.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail and food services sales (RSAFS) exceed $600 billion, consumer spending is exceptionally robust, signaling strong economic momentum that supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of months when RSAFS exceeds the threshold.
